prevalence.source: Ministry of Health STI surveillance; WHO syphilis context
prevalence.trend: stable to decreasing with expanded screening and treatment

incidence.value: Very low; late progression from untreated infection

burden.dalyPerCapita: low population burden; high per-case morbidity (neurosyphilis, cardiovascular syphilis)
burden.mortalityRate: Low to moderate in advanced cardiovascular/neuro disease

vulnerablePopulations: older adults with long-standing untreated infection
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Reactive treponemal test plus compatible late manifestations (neuro/cardiovascular/gummatous); CSF testing for neurosyphilis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: expanded screening in prenatal and STI services
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with timely penicillin therapy and follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Serology widely available; neurosyphilis evaluation in referral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: IV penicillin G for neurosyphilis; benzathine penicillin for late syphilis; monitor serologic response
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Late presentation and loss to follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Penicillin widely available and structured STI programs
